name: Scientific-Humanitarian Committee
description: The world's first gay rights organization, founded by Magnus Hirschfeld in Berlin. Campaigned to repeal Paragraph 175 (which criminalised homosexuality in Germany) through scientific argument and public petitions. Dissolved when the Nazi regime destroyed Hirschfeld's Institut für Sexualwissenschaft in 1933.

name: Mattachine Society
description: One of the earliest sustained gay rights organizations in the United States, founded by Harry Hay in Los Angeles. The group provided community support and early advocacy for gay rights during intense police repression and McCarthyite persecution. The name comes from medieval French court jesters who spoke truth to power.

name: Daughters of Bilitis
description: The first lesbian civil and political rights organization in the United States, founded by Del Martin and Phyllis Lyon in San Francisco. It published 'The Ladder' (1956–1972), the first nationally distributed lesbian periodical in the US, and provided community and advocacy for lesbians at a time of extreme social repression.

name: Gay Liberation Front
description: Founded in New York two weeks after the Stonewall Uprising in 1969, the GLF represented a new, militant approach to LGBTQ+ rights rooted in the broader liberation politics of the era. It organized the first Christopher Street Liberation Day March (1970) and inspired similar groups worldwide, including the UK Gay Liberation Front (1970).

name: PFLAG
description: Founded by Jeanne Manford in 1973 after she marched with her gay son in New York's Pride parade. The first and largest organization for LGBTQ+ people, their families, and allies in the US. Operates over 400 chapters in all 50 states and several countries.

name: Lambda Legal
description: The oldest and largest US legal organization working for the civil rights of LGBTQ+ people and those living with HIV. Won or contributed to landmark cases including Romer v. Evans (1996), Lawrence v. Texas (2003), and Obergefell v. Hodges (2015).

name: National LGBTQ Task Force
description: America's oldest LGBTQ+ advocacy organization. Organizes the annual Creating Change conference, publishes research on LGBTQ+ issues, and works on grassroots organizing and progressive policy. Achieved the removal of homosexuality from the APA DSM within months of founding.

name: Human Rights Campaign
description: The largest LGBTQ+ civil rights organization in the United States. Known for its Corporate Equality Index, which rates companies on LGBTQ+ workplace inclusion, and for federal legislative advocacy. Has over 3 million members and supporters.

name: GLAAD
description: Originally the Gay & Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation, founded to protest sensationalized AIDS coverage in the New York Post. Works through entertainment, news, and digital media to advance LGBTQ+ acceptance. Publishes the annual Studio Responsibility Index and TV LGBTQ+ inclusion report.

name: ACLU LGBTQ+ & HIV Project
description: The ACLU's dedicated LGBTQ+ and HIV rights project pursues litigation, advocacy, and public education on a broad range of issues. Has challenged anti-trans legislation, defended same-sex relationships, and worked on marriage equality, adoption rights, and military service.

name: GLSEN
description: Works to create safe and inclusive K–12 school environments for LGBTQ+ students. Supports a national network of GSA (Gender and Sexuality Alliance) clubs, conducts the National School Climate Survey, and provides educator training resources.

name: It Gets Better Project
description: Founded by Dan Savage and Terry Miller in response to a wave of LGBTQ+ youth suicides. Produces and curates video stories of LGBTQ+ adults telling young people their lives will improve. Has collected over 60,000 user-created videos viewed more than 50 million times.

name: National Center for Transgender Equality
description: The leading social justice advocacy organization advancing policy change for transgender people in the US. Produces the US Transgender Survey, the largest known survey of trans people in the world, and advocates for federal policy protections.

name: ILGA World
description: The International Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Trans and Intersex Association — a worldwide federation of over 1,700 member organizations in more than 160 countries. Holds ECOSOC consultative status at the United Nations. Publishes the annual State-Sponsored Homophobia report.

name: ILGA-Europe
description: European regional organisation of ILGA World. Advocates for LGBTQ+ rights across 49 European countries and publishes the annual Rainbow Europe map, ranking countries on a 0–100% equality scale based on legal protections and policies.

name: Stonewall UK
description: The largest LGBTQ+ charity in the UK and Europe. Founded in 1989 in response to Section 28 by activists including Ian McKellen and Michael Cashman. Known for the Workplace Equality Index and campaigns resulting in civil partnerships (2004) and same-sex marriage (2013).

name: OutRight Action International
description: Advocates for the human rights of LGBTIQ+ people globally through the United Nations system. Monitors UN processes, supports civil society in the Global South, and works to advance LGBTIQ+ inclusion in the international human rights framework.

name: Gay, Lesbian and Straight Education Network (GLBTQ Legal Advocates & Defenders)
description: New England's leading LGBTQ+ legal rights organization. Litigated Goodridge v. Department of Public Health (2003), resulting in Massachusetts becoming the first US state to legalise same-sex marriage. Provides a free Legal InfoLine for LGBTQ+ legal questions.

name: Matthew Shepard Foundation
description: Founded by Judy and Dennis Shepard after their son Matthew was murdered in a hate crime in 1998. Promotes acceptance of LGBTQ+ people and combats hate crimes through youth advocacy and the Erase Hate program. Instrumental in passing the Matthew Shepard and James Byrd Jr. Hate Crimes Prevention Act (2009).

name: Amnesty International — LGBTQ+ Rights
description: Since 1991, Amnesty International campaigns for the release of anyone imprisoned solely for their sexual orientation or gender identity, treating such imprisonment as a prisoner-of-conscience violation. Documents criminalisation in 60+ countries and campaigns for universal decriminalisation.

name: Human Rights Watch — LGBTQ+ Rights
description: Human Rights Watch documents abuses against LGBTQ+ people in over 70 countries, including criminalisation, state-sponsored violence, and discrimination. Provides detailed investigative reports used by UN bodies and advocates for legal reforms globally.

name: National Queer and Trans Therapists of Color Network
description: A healing justice organization committed to transforming mental health for queer and trans people of color. Maintains a therapist directory for QTPOC, distributes mental health fund grants, and organises around collective healing and liberation.

name: AVEN (Asexual Visibility and Education Network)
description: The largest online community and educational resource for asexual, aromantic, and questioning people. Founded by David Jay. Hosts the world's largest asexual community forum and provides informational resources for people exploring their identities.

name: Intersex Human Rights Australia
description: An intersex-led organization advocating for the human rights of intersex people in Australia and internationally. Created the intersex pride symbol (2013) and participates in UN treaty body processes. Advocates against non-consensual medical interventions on intersex children.

name: Metropolitan Community Church
description: The first Christian denomination founded specifically to serve LGBTQ+ communities. Founded by Rev. Troy Perry in Los Angeles. Has congregations in 35+ countries and has performed same-sex commitment ceremonies since its founding, predating legal recognition by decades.

name: GLAAD UK (Broken Rainbow UK / Galop)
description: Galop is the UK's LGBTQ+ anti-violence charity. Provides specialist support to LGBTQ+ people who have experienced hate crime, domestic abuse, or sexual violence. Campaigns for improved police responses and justice system treatment of LGBTQ+ victims.

name: Sage (Services and Advocacy for GLBT Elders)
description: The world's oldest and largest organization dedicated to LGBTQ+ older adults. Provides direct services, advocacy, and operates the SAGE National LGBT Elder Hotline (1-877-360-5428). Addresses isolation, housing discrimination, and healthcare disparities affecting LGBTQ+ seniors.
